storage.value:
Building an authentication system

PRD outlines login, MFA, password reset flows, and foundational security posture for Account Services.

User login

  • Email/password entry with secure session/token lifecycle.
  • Brute-force and rate-limit protection on auth endpoints.
  • Optional future SSO adapter (out of MVP scope).
  • Journey: Login with valid credentials — session establishes, dashboard landing.
  • Journey: Login with invalid credentials — generic error, no oracle; lockout thresholds documented.
  • Journey: Login with MFA — challenge follows primary authentication; cancel/back preserves no partial privileged session.

Multi-factor authentication

  • MFA enrollment and removal UX.
  • TOTP-first with WebAuthn follow-on in roadmap framing.
  • Backup codes policy and audit trails for MFA events.

Password reset

  • Timeboxed single-use reset tokens emailed to verified channel.
  • Interactions with MFA and lockouts; safe user-facing wording.
  • Observe password policy on reset completion.

Security constraints

  • Hashed credential storage aligning with accepted ADR baseline.
  • HTTPS-only transport with HSTS for web clients.
  • Secrets via managed vault—not configuration files.
  • C4-aligned architecture: Auth Gateway ↔ Session Service ↔ Identity store.
